topic.name: Infrastructure and Landmarks

Document 1: The Grand Palace is a complex of buildings at the heart of Bangkok, Thailand.

Document 2: The Dartford Crossing is a major road crossing of the River Thames in England.
It has been described as one of the most important road crossings in Britain.
Document 3: Wilkins Peak is a small mountain located in Sweetwater County, Wyoming.
The mountain is also a popular location for mountain biking and hiking.
Document 4: M-211 is a state trunkline highway in the Lower Peninsula of Michigan.
The highway runs through farmland and forests along the 5.174-mile route to the park.
Document 5: M-189 is a north – south state trunkline highway in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan.
The highway runs to the northeast away from the river through forests.
Document 6: F-41 is a county-designated highway in the Lower Peninsula of Michigan.

topic.name: Atlantic Hurricane Impacts
topic.description: The documents discuss various hurricanes that formed in the Atlantic, detailing their development, intensity, and impacts on land and communities.

Document 1: The effects of Hurricane Dennis in Florida included 14 deaths and $1.5 billion in damage.
Dennis rapidly intensified to attain Category 4 status on the Saffir-Simpson Hurricane Scale.
Document 2: Hurricane Debby was the strongest tropical cyclone of the 1982 Atlantic hurricane season.
Debby briefly peaked as a Category 4 hurricane on the Saffir – Simpson hurricane Scale.
Document 4: The 1864 Atlantic hurricane season was the third consecutive Atlantic hurricane season with no hurricane landfall in the United States.
Of the five known 1864 cyclones, four were first documented in 1995 by Jose Fernandez-Partagas and Henry Diaz.
Document 5: Hurricane Arlene was the first tropical cyclone of the 1963 Atlantic hurricane season and one of the wettest storms ever recorded in Bermuda.
Along its track, several hurricane warnings and watches were issued for the Leeward Islands.
Document 6: Hurricane Linda was a strong tropical cyclone in September 2015 that resulted in heavy rains across portions of Mexico and the Southwestern United States.
In Mexico, the storm brought rainfall to nine states, causing flooding, especially in Oaxaca, Sinaloa, and Zacatecas.
Document 7: Tropical Depression One-E was the earliest known tropical cyclone to impact the Mexican state of Sinaloa.
High winds accompanied the rainfall and left about 50,000 residences without power.

topic.name: Fungal Species Descriptions
topic.description: The documents provide detailed descriptions of various fungal species, including their taxonomy, morphology, habitat, and edibility.

Document 1: Leotia lubrica, commonly referred to as a jelly baby, is a species of fungus in the family Leotiaceae.
The species feeds as a saprotroph.
Document 2: Lactarius alnicola, commonly known as the golden milkcap, is a species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
The fungus is found in the western United States and Mexico.
Document 3: Leucopholiota decorosa is a species of fungus in the Tricholomataceae family of mushrooms.
It is saprobic, growing on the decaying wood of hardwood trees.
Document 4: Phellodon is a genus of tooth fungi in the family Bankeraceae.
Species grow in a symbiotic mycorrhizal association with trees from the families Fagaceae and Pinaceae.
Document 6: Wynnea americana, commonly known as moose antlers or rabbit ears, is a species of fungus in the family Sarcoscyphaceae.
The fruit bodies grow clustered together from large underground masses of compacted mycelia known as sclerotia.
Document 7: Spongiforma squarepantsii is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family, genus Spongiforma.
It produces sponge-like, rubbery orange fruit bodies that have a fruity or musky odour.

topic.name: Medieval Fortifications in England
topic.description: The documents discuss various medieval castles in England, their historical significance, construction, and evolution over time as military and administrative structures.

Document 1: Skipsea Castle is a Norman motte and bailey castle near the village of Skipsea, East Riding of Yorkshire, England.
The remains of the castle had little value by the end of the 14th century and Skipsea Brough failed to attract many inhabitants.
Document 2: St Catherine's Castle is a Henrician castle in Cornwall, built by Thomas Treffry between approximately 1538 and 1540.
The castle remained in use for many years until it was closed at the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815.
Document 3: Okehampton Castle is a medieval motte and bailey castle in Devon, England.
It continued in use as a fortification until the late 13th century.
Document 4: Berkhamsted Castle is a Norman motte-and-bailey castle in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire.
The castle was besieged in 1216 during the civil war between King John and rebellious barons.
Document 5: Conwy Castle is a medieval fortification in Conwy, on the north coast of Wales.
Over the next few centuries, the castle played an important part in several wars.
Document 6: Netley Castle is a former artillery fort constructed in either 1542 or 1544 by Henry VIII.
It formed part of the King 's Device programme to protect against invasion from France and the Holy Roman Empire.
Document 7: Sandsfoot Castle, also known historically as Weymouth Castle, is an artillery fort constructed by Henry VIII near Weymouth, Dorset.
The castle saw service during the English Civil War, when it was held by Parliament and Royalists in turn.

Most of the children involved with prostitution are girls, despite an increase in the number of young boys in the trade.
Document 3: Struggles for Freedom of Expression in American History is a non-fiction book about the history of freedom of speech in the United States.
The history of free speech shows the need for broadly protective free speech rules applied generally and equally.
Document 4: A More Perfect Union: Advancing New American Rights examines race as it relates to African Americans in American history.
The book presents a North-South schism lens through which to view the congressional politics of race.
Document 5: The Colorado recall election of 2013 was a successful effort to recall two Democratic members of the Colorado Senate following their support of new gun control legislation.
National organizations on both the gun rights and gun control sides became involved by providing mailings and donations.
Document 6: Tax protesters in the United States advance a number of constitutional arguments asserting that the imposition, assessment and collection of the federal income tax violates the United States Constitution.
Some protesters argue that imposition of income taxes violates the First Amendment freedom of speech.
Document 7: Davis v. Ayala was a case in which the Supreme Court upheld a death sentence of a Hispanic defendant despite the fact that all Blacks and Hispanics were excluded from the jury.
Ayala filed several appeals challenging the constitutionality of the trial court's decision to exclude his counsel from the hearings.

topic.name: British Railway Locomotives
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of British locomotives, their designs, historical significance, and operational roles within the railway system.

Document 1: The SR Q1 class is a type of austerity steam locomotive constructed during the Second World War.
A total of 40 locomotives were built.
Document 2: The LSWR M7 class is a class of 0-4-4 passenger tank locomotive built between 1897 and 1911.
105 were built and the class went through several modifications over five production batches.
Document 3: The General Electric steam turbine locomotives were two steam turbine locomotives built by General Electric for Union Pacific in 1938.
They completed test runs and participated in a variety of publicity events for the railroad.
Document 5: The British Rail Class 47 is a class of British railway diesel-electric locomotive that was developed in the 1960s by Brush Traction.
A total of 512 Class 47s were built at Crewe Works and Brush's Falcon Works.

topic.name: Corporate History and Mergers
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the corporate histories, mergers, and significant events of various companies across different industries.

Owen's success as a lawyer in 1906 in winning a major court case on behalf of the Eastern Cherokees.
2: Salesforce Marketing Cloud was founded under the name ExactTarget in late 2000
It was acquired by Salesforce for $2.5 billion in 2013.

In 1998, SBC merged with Union Bank of Switzerland to form UBS.

The bank continued to grow through acquisitions.

topic.name: Philosophical Arguments for God
topic.description: The documents discuss various philosophical arguments and theories related to the existence of God and the problem of evil.

Document 1: Irenaean theodicy is a Christian theodicy designed to respond to the problem of evil.
the Irenaean theodicy asserts that the world is the best of all possible worlds because it allows humans to fully develop.
Document 2: An ontological argument is a philosophical argument for the existence of God that uses ontology.
Anselm defined God as 'that than which nothing greater can be conceived'.
Document 7: Kantian ethics refers to a deontological ethical theory ascribed to the German philosopher Immanuel Kant.
Central to Kant's construction of the moral law is the categorical imperative.

topic.name: Texas Coastal Ecosystems
topic.description: The documents discuss various bays in Texas, highlighting their ecological significance, historical context, and human interactions.

Document 1: St. Charles Bay is an inlet of Aransas Bay in Aransas County, Texas.
The bay hosts a valued ecosystem for the endangered whooping crane.
Document 2: Copano Bay is a northwestern extension of Aransas Bay, home to a diverse ecosystem.
It is classified as a nursery for shrimp, which prohibits production from the bay.
Document 4: Nueces Bay is a natural estuary, ecologically and economically vital to the surrounding area.
The bay serves as a habitat for the propagation of fish and shellfish, which sustain diverse species of birds and other wildlife.

topic.name: Religious Institutions and Architecture
topic.description: The documents discuss various religious institutions, their histories, architectural styles, and the communities they serve.

Document 1: Bath Abbey is an Anglican parish church and a former Benedictine monastery in Bath, Somerset, England.
It is one of the largest examples of Perpendicular Gothic architecture in the West Country.
Document 2: Congregation Beth Israel is a Modern Orthodox synagogue located in Louisiana.
It is the oldest Orthodox congregation in the New Orleans region.
Document 4: The Church of the Holy Mother of God is a medieval Eastern Orthodox church in the village of Donja Kamenica.
The church is notable for its unusual architectural style.
Document 5: McAllister Tower Apartments is a residential apartment skyscraper that was initially conceived as a large church.
The building opened in 1930 as the William Taylor Hotel and Temple Methodist Episcopal Church.
Document 6: Temple Israel is a Reform congregation located at 130 Riverside Drive in Dayton, Ohio.
The congregation constructed a larger synagogue building in 1927.
Document 7: Gene Robinson is an American retired bishop of the Diocese of New Hampshire in the Episcopal Church.
Robinson is widely known for being the first priest in an openly gay relationship to be consecrated a bishop.

topic.name: Television Episodes and Characters
topic.description: This cluster focuses on notable television episodes and characters from various series, highlighting their plots, character development, and cultural impact.

The series depicts the everyday lives of office employees in the Scranton, Pennsylvania branch of the fictional Dunder Mifflin Paper Company.
Document 2: Daphne Clarke is a fictional character from the Australian soap opera Neighbours, played by Elaine Smith.

The show centers on FBI special agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully who work on cases linked to the paranormal.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Influences
topic.description: This topic encompasses the exploration of cultural, historical, and linguistic elements that shape societies and their expressions.

Its building was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2016.
Document 2: Written Chinese comprises Chinese characters used to represent the Chinese language.
Chinese characters have evolved into well-developed styles of Chinese calligraphy.
Document 3: Whorf is widely known as an advocate for the idea that because of linguistic differences in grammar and usage, speakers of different languages conceptualize and experience the world differently.
This principle has frequently been called the 'Sapir – Whorf hypothesis'.

Document 6: Hamdi Qandil is a prominent Egyptian journalist, news anchor, talk show host and activist.
Qandil started his journalism career in the 1950s when he wrote for the Akher Sa 'a magazine.
Document 7: The One I Love is a romantic, slice-of-life shōjo manga by Clamp, an all-female, manga artist team.
The manga contains twelve independent stories, each focusing on an aspect of love.

included on the New Jersey Register of Historic Places and the National Register of Historic Places in 1973.

failed financially after beginning construction.

the oldest lighthouse in Queensland.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Significance
topic.description: The documents explore various cultural and historical elements ranging from music, politics, sports, to natural resources.

Document 1: True Blue is a song by American singer Madonna.
The song deals with the feelings of Madonna for her then-husband Sean Penn.
Document 2: The lily family, Liliaceae, consists of fifteen genera and about 705 known species of flowering plants.
Many Liliaceae are important ornamental plants.

Borjomi was made into a top Soviet export.
Document 4: Maria Rosaria 'Mara' Carfagna is an Italian politician and former showgirl.
She served as Minister for Equal Opportunity in Berlusconi IV Cabinet.
Document 5: UFC 94 was a mixed martial arts pay-per-view event promoted by the Ultimate Fighting Championship.
The main event featured the UFC Welterweight Champion defending his title.
Document 6: 'Sandwich Day' is an episode of the television series 30 Rock.
The episode earned Tina Fey the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series.
Document 7: The Pacers – Pistons brawl was an altercation that occurred in an NBA game.
The fight led the NBA to increase security between players and fans.

topic.name: Scientology Controversies and Protests
topic.description: This topic encompasses the various protests, legal battles, and internet censorship controversies surrounding the Church of Scientology.

Document 1: Project Chanology was a protest movement against the practices of the Church of Scientology by members of Anonymous.
The project was started in response to the Church of Scientology's attempts to remove material from a highly publicized interview with Scientologist Tom Cruise from the Internet.
Document 2: The Haymarket affair was the aftermath of a bombing that took place at a labor demonstration in support of workers striking for an eight-hour day.
The Haymarket affair is generally considered significant as the origin of international May Day observances for workers.
Document 4: A series of incidents in 2009 led to Church of Scientology-owned networks being banned from making edits to Wikipedia articles relating to Scientology.
The Church of Scientology has long had a controversial history on the Internet, and has initiated campaigns to manipulate material and remove information critical of itself from the web.

topic.name: Fictional Characters in TV Shows

Document 2: Jenny is a fictional character in the long-running British science fiction television series Doctor Who.
Jenny was introduced by writer Stephen Greenhorn.
Document 4: Tempest is the twenty-first episode and season finale of the first season of the WB original series Smallville.
The episode's narrative involves Lex attempting to forge his own destiny away from LuthorCorp.
Document 5: Roy's Wedding is the second episode of the ninth season of the American comedy television series The Office.
The episode depicts the everyday lives of office employees in the Scranton, Pennsylvania branch of the fictional Dunder Mifflin Paper Company.
Document 6: Night Terrors is the ninth episode of the sixth series of the British science fiction television series Doctor Who.
The episode features the Doctor and his companions dealing with a young boy's fears.
Document 7: Donna Freedman is a fictional character from the Australian soap opera Neighbours, played by Margot Robbie.
Donna was initially introduced as a guest character, but Robbie was promoted to the regular cast shortly after she made her debut.
